description_long: This electric fan features a triangular, flat top housing with a smooth, matte finish, supported by a central vertical column. Three elongated, slightly curved blades extend outward from the central hub, each blade appearing thin and slightly reflective. The fan lacks a visible blade guard, leaving the blades exposed and giving it a minimalist, industrial look. The base is compact and sturdy, designed to support the fan's weight and maintain stability. No power cord or control panel is visible, suggesting a streamlined, possibly remote-operated or automated design. The overall color palette is dominated by shades of gray, with subtle differences in tone between the blades and the housing.
description: A minimalist electric fan with a triangular top, three exposed blades, and a central support column, all in shades of gray. The design is sleek and industrial, with no visible controls or power cord.
description_view_0: Front view shows the triangular top, central support, and three blades extending outward, with the left blade most visible.
description_view_1: Angled view highlights the thickness of the top housing and the curvature of the blades, with the right blade more prominent.
description_view_2: Rear view centers the support column and shows the symmetrical arrangement of the three blades.
description_view_3: Another angled view, emphasizing the right side of the fan and the exposed blade edges.
attribute_blade_guard_design: No blade guard is present; the blades are fully exposed, giving the fan an open-frame, minimalist appearance.
